notes: File-level storage connected to network; Snap Appliance's core product category
Inexpensive ATA disks used in Snap Server appliances as alternative to SCSI; superseded by SATA and SSD
Redundant Array of Independent Disks; Snap Servers offered RAID 1 and RAID 5 configurations
Proprietary OS for Snap Server workgroup products; field-hardened for ease of use
Linux-based OS for departmental and enterprise Snap Servers; added snapshots and Windows ADS support
Automated scheduled client backup software included with every Snap Server; unlimited user license
Storage tier between high-performance SCSI and tape; Aberdeen identified as significant cost-saving opportunity
High-speed network connectivity; Snap Server 4200/4500 included dual gigabit-Ethernet adapters
